title: Longest Consecutive Sequence
description: Given an unsorted array of integers nums, return the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence. You must implement the solution in O(n) time complexity. Your solution should utilize a set to keep track of the numbers and check for consecutive sequences efficiently.

examples.explanation: The longest consecutive sequence is [1, 2, 3, 4, 100]. Therefore, the length is 5.
The longest consecutive sequence is [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]. Therefore, the length is 5.
The longest consecutive sequence is [0, 1, 2]. Therefore, the length is 3.

constraints: The length of nums will be in the range [0, 10^4].
Each element in nums is a signed 32-bit integer.
hints: Consider using a set to store the elements for O(1) lookups.
Try to iterate through the numbers while avoiding duplicates to improve efficiency.
Think about how you can find the start of a new sequence.
Use a variable to keep track of the maximum length found during iteration.
